is an Australian record producer, audio engineer, sound mixer and multi-instrumentalist. He has received five [ARIA Artisan Awards](/wiki/ARIA_Music_Awards "ARIA Music Awards") and has produced over 50 albums. McKercher has worked with Australian artists [Josh Pyke](/wiki/Josh_Pyke "Josh Pyke"), [Bertie Blackman](/wiki/Bertie_Blackman "Bertie Blackman"), [Papa vs Pretty](/wiki/Papa_vs_Pretty "Papa vs Pretty"), [Sarah Blasko](/wiki/Sarah_Blasko "Sarah Blasko"), [You Am I](/wiki/You_Am_I "You Am I"), [Pete Murray](/wiki/Pete_Murray_(Australian_singer-songwriter) "Pete Murray (Australian singer-songwriter)"), [Motor Ace](/wiki/Motor_Ace "Motor Ace") and [Eskimo Joe](/wiki/Eskimo_Joe "Eskimo Joe"). An avowed analogue fan, he specialises in the use of tape, although he also uses digital technologies.

Musical artist

**Paul McKercher** is an Australian record producer, audio engineer, sound mixer and multi-instrumentalist. He has received five [ARIA Artisan Awards](/wiki/ARIA_Music_Awards "ARIA Music Awards") and has produced over 50 albums. McKercher has worked with Australian artists [Josh Pyke](/wiki/Josh_Pyke "Josh Pyke"), [Bertie Blackman](/wiki/Bertie_Blackman "Bertie Blackman"), [Papa vs Pretty](/wiki/Papa_vs_Pretty "Papa vs Pretty"), [Sarah Blasko](/wiki/Sarah_Blasko "Sarah Blasko"), [You Am I](/wiki/You_Am_I "You Am I"), [Pete Murray](/wiki/Pete_Murray_(Australian_singer-songwriter) "Pete Murray (Australian singer-songwriter)"), [Motor Ace](/wiki/Motor_Ace "Motor Ace") and [Eskimo Joe](/wiki/Eskimo_Joe "Eskimo Joe"). An avowed analogue fan, he specialises in the use of tape, although he also uses digital technologies.

## Biography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Paul_McKercher&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Biography")]

Paul McKercher worked at national youth radio station, [Triple J](/wiki/Triple_J "Triple J"), for three years. In 1991 he and fellow audio engineer John Jacobs created the "JJJ News Theme" as a mix of McKercher's guitar playing, a scratch of N.W.A.'s "[Fuck tha Police](/wiki/Fuck_tha_Police "Fuck tha Police")" and the orchestral [ABC News](/wiki/ABC_News_(Australia) "ABC News (Australia)") theme, "[Majestic Fanfare](/wiki/Majestic_Fanfare "Majestic Fanfare")", with the beat based on Prince's "[Gett Off](/wiki/Gett_Off "Gett Off")".[[1]](#cite_note-Nuttall-1)[[2]](#cite_note-JJJ_Interview-2) McKercher followed with seven years at [ABC radio](/wiki/ABC_Radio_National "ABC Radio National").[[3]](#cite_note-Song_May_12-3) He has also worked as a free-lance record producer, engineer and mixer since 1992. For three weeks in 1993 McKercher recorded indie pop group [Falling Joys](/wiki/Falling_Joys "Falling Joys")' third studio album *Aerial* (August 1993).[[4]](#cite_note-McFarlane-4)[[5]](#cite_note-Times_Aug_93-5) Using three different [eight-track machines](/wiki/Multitrack_recording "Multitrack recording"), at an isolated house in [Kangaroo Valley](/wiki/Kangaroo_Valley,_New_South_Wales "Kangaroo Valley, New South Wales"), he captured the band live-in-the-studio.[[5]](#cite_note-Times_Aug_93-5)

In late 1997 Kercher was working at [Woodstock Studios](/wiki/Woodstock_Studios "Woodstock Studios"), Melbourne on pop rock band the Jaynes' [extended play](/wiki/Extended_play "Extended play") *Dogbone* (April 1998).[[6]](#cite_note-Holmgren-6)[[7]](#cite_note-Times_Jan_97-7) He has written articles for *Audio Technology Magazine*.[[3]](#cite_note-Song_May_12-3) McKercher's techniques were discussed by Zolton Zavos in May 2000 in part one of "Making Tracks" in *[Tharunka](/wiki/Tharunka "Tharunka")* on his work at Megaphon Studios, [Petersham](/wiki/Petersham,_New_South_Wales "Petersham, New South Wales").[[8]](#cite_note-Zavos-8) Zavos acknowledged his "wonderful ear" when he worked as a producer, engineer and multi-instrumentalist.[[8]](#cite_note-Zavos-8)

In 2010 he addressed the Face the Music Conference.[[9]](#cite_note-Face_2010-9) As from December 2016 he was a teacher at [SAE Institute](/wiki/SAE_Institute "SAE Institute"), Sydney delivering classes on "how to produce recordings" as well as continuing as a producer, engineer and mixer.[[10]](#cite_note-Scenestr_Dec_16-10)

## Notes

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Paul_McKercher&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Notes")]

1. **[^](#cite_ref-18)** For the US market [The Anyones](/wiki/The_Anyones "The Anyones") (and their 2003 self-titled album) were renamed as the Everyones due to a naming conflict with Los Angeles band [Anyone](/wiki/Anyone_(band) "Anyone (band)").[[17]](#cite_note-Renaming-17) Consequently [AllMusic](/wiki/AllMusic "AllMusic") has the release as *The Everyones* by the Everyones, in 2004.[[11]](#cite_note-AMG_Creds-11)
